entry.title: Re: Po Related Variable in Flexible Workflow Body and Sub...
entry.content: /IWPGW/BADI_TGW_TASK_DATA, the other is the interface

entry.title: Re: Can you capitalise an asset with depreciation?
entry.content: Try ABSOL with TTY158, go to LINE ITEMS and you can specify Prior year APC and Deprec.

entry.title: Webinar Alert : Delivering Seamless S/4HANA Transformation for Oil and Gas
entry.content:
Delivering Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transformation for Oil and Gas Companies

Join our KaarTech webinar series if you are looking for ways to ease the pressure to modernize your ERP solution landscape
 
Are you grappling with legacy ERP systems that hinder growth and efficiency? Is data chaos and operational inefficiency keeping you awake at night? You're not alone. Many organizations face these challenges daily. That's why KaarTech invites you to join our exclusive webinar series where we delve into the transformative journey of a major oil and gas company.
 
Addressing Pain Points :
  • Overwhelmed by Legacy Systems: Learn how to overcome the hurdles posed by outdated ERP systems that fragment operations.
  • Data Chaos: Discover strategies to tackle data silos and integrity issues, crucial for accurate decision-making amidst diverse data sources.
  • Resistance to Change: Navigate through team resistance with proven tactics to make your S/4HANA transition smoother.
Why Attend? This 3-part series goes beyond theory to provide actionable insights and strategies tailored for SAP customers worldwide. Each session equips you with practical insights for a risk-free S/4HANA transformation from ERP core analysis to Tech Debt mitigation and change management.
 
Key Sessions Include:
  • Part 1 - Analyzing Your Existing SAP Investments and Assessing Technical Debt
  • Part 2 - Data Migration Strategies in Preparation for Transformation
  • Part 3 - Managing Your Organization's Resistance To Change
By the series end, you'll have:
  • A clear roadmap to improve your ERP core.
  • Strategies to optimize edge systems for enhanced supply chain efficiency.
  • Best practices for data management crucial for S/4HANA readiness.
  • Actionable insights into effective change management.
Join us for an insightful journey as we decode the path to SAP S/4HANA transformation. This isn't just another webinar; it's your guide to overcoming challenges and seizing opportunities in the digital era.
 
REGISTER NOW

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Oil, Gas, and Energy Digital Technologies Digital Transformation

entry.title: Re: SAP AppGyver x Firebase① Google Firebaseとの連携
entry.content: Where is CLIENT_ID? I can't find this CLIENT_ID in GoogleService-Info.plist.

entry.title: How to generate e-way bill output form in s4 Hana public cloud
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have a requirement to provide the E-way bill output form in S4 Hana public cloud with below details but we couldn't found any output forms to display and print the data.

E-waybill Details, Address Details (Dispatch from), To Location, Goods details and QR code.

Can you please help, Where we can see the standard output form and customisation options.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: sap log showing out of memory
entry.content:
Hi,
While checking for the AR Invoice logs there was an error showing "out of memory".
Can anyone help me to figure out the solution.
Do we need to upgrade it to sap 64bit or can we run on the same ?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Customer service process tutorials for inhouse proces...
entry.content:
Hello,

Here are comprehensive resources for Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud-service

For In-house repair specifically, here are some resources:

Blog: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview

Demo: S/4HANA Cloud: In-House Repair with Customer Returns

Warm regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: SAP公有云自动开票集成
entry.content:
你好，

是的

参考文档

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance:Electronic Invoicing for China 

金税发票销项管理百望云直连Cloud Demo 

Regards,

Sen

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - How to estimate the remaining storage free space?
entry.content:
Hi @antoniojmnunes 

I am surprised that there was no mention of the instance monitoring extension API in this post, which imho is much more flexible and secure than connecting directly to the postgres instance

https://pages.github.tools.sap/cloudservices/docs/postgresql/Instance-Monitoring/

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing - subscribe to MQTT topics
entry.content:
Hi Fabian,

I cant comment on the error message (you might have to raise a support ticket for that) but assume you have set up Mosquitto with client credential authentication as well.

What I can tell you is that I looked at out internal DM roadmap and it is planned to relax the MQTT authentication in the 2411 release (subject to change) so that you can connect to brokers without authentication.

Thanks

Kevin

entry.title: Financial Statements from SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

My boss taught me when we want to get financial statements, we use the "F.01".

For examples, If I want to get the data of only July.

In this case, we need to run the following two reports by F.01. 

1) From January to June

2) From January to July

And exports these data as excel fines then 2) less 1) = July data 

I cannot believe in it as SAP cannot get single month data...

Is this story true? If not, please kindly teach me the way if possible.

Thank you in Advance for your help.

entry.title: ABSL syntax to make attachment necessary for specific business users only with the use of SDK
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Can anyone help me to write .absl syntax to make attachment necessary for specific business users (sales emp and sales manager) only with the use of SDK.

I have below logic but don't know how to write code with this.

this.result = Context.GetCurrentUserUISwitches();

Thank you!!!

 

 

Regards,

Dhaval Solanki

entry.title: Deployment cap application with Fiori UIhttps://community.sap.com/t5/forums/editpage/board-id/websit
entry.content:
Hi Community,

How to deploy cap application with Fiori UI and also using destination 

I am trying to call S4 Hana API in mta.yaml

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: StonemanPortal
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 9.0.0
modules:
- name: StonemanPortal-srv
type: nodejs
path: gen/srv
requires:
- name: StonemanPortal-db
- name: StonemanPortal-auth
provides:
- name: srv-api
properties:
srv-url: ${default-url}
parameters:
buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
readiness-health-check-type: http
build-parameters:
builder: npm
- name: StonemanPortal
type: approuter.nodejs
path: app/router
requires:
- name: srv-api
group: destinations
properties:
forwardAuthToken: true
name: srv-api
url: ~{srv-url}
- name: StonemanPortal-auth
- name: S4HANA_QA
group: destinations
properties:
forwardAuthToken: false
name: ui5
url: https://ui5.sap.com
parameters:
disk-quota: 256M
keep-existing-routes: true
memory: 256M
- name: StonemanPortal-app-content
type: com.sap.application.content
path: .
requires:
- name: StonemanPortal_html_repo_host
parameters:
content-target: true
build-parameters:
build-result: resources
requires:
- artifacts:
- stoneman.zip
name: stoneman
target-path: resources/
- name: stoneman
type: html5
path: app/stoneman
build-parameters:
build-result: dist
builder: custom
commands:
- npm install
- npm run build:cf
supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: StonemanPortal-auth
type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
parameters:
config:
tenant-mode: dedicated
xsappname: StonemanPortal-${org}-${space}
path: ./xs-security.json
service: xsuaa
service-plan: application
- name: StonemanPortal-db
type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
parameters:
service: hana
service-plan: hdi-shared
- name: StonemanPortal_html_repo_host
type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
parameters:
service: html5-apps-repo
service-plan: app-host
- name: S4HANA_QA
type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
parameters:
config:
HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
init_data:
instance:
destinations:
- Authentication: BasicAuthentication
User: SAPBTP
Password: xPFgdyoHtfyxomKqnKx#vZSeRL6LGWEgAzcJRGPN
Name: S4HANA_QA
ProxyType: Internet
Type: HTTP
URL: https://my409722-api.s4hana.cloud.sap
existing_destinations_policy: update
version: 1.0.0
service: destination
service-name: stoneman
service-plan: lite
parameters:
deploy_mode: html5-repo
enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
before-all:
- builder: custom
commands:
- npm ci
- npx cds build --production

 

Thank's

Durgesh1685

entry.title: EDOC_COCKPIT invoice document submitted, status stays "In process"
entry.content:
When we try to submit an invoice in the EDOC_COCKPIT the status is yellow. If I hover over the yellow triangle it says "in process" and the status description says "Req. sent to EWC". We are currently testing a change that involves the determination of a product code in the XML, so it is on our Q-system. Is there a system setting that may prevent edocs from being sent from Q like e-mails from SOST? I am totally new to the EDOC subject, so still many things I don't know. I also tried EDOC_BACKGROUND, but no change in status. Our system is still ECC not S/4 HANA yet.
Attached see some screenshots

Also I am new to SCN posting questions. So, sorry, if tags are too vague/wrong.

entry.title: How to run full stack cap project locally with calling s4 HANA api using destination
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I have Full Stack CAP application. 

below Scenario we faced 1.

1.if we run the project through cmd - cds watch --profile hybrid , then API/UI works correctly. but S4 hana api gives 404 not found.

2. If we run the UI project seprately through cmd - npm run start. then UI works correctly along with S4 hana api, but odata services api gives 404 not found.

3. when we call hardcoded base URL of S4 hana api . through cmd - cds watch --profile hybrid then S4 hana api gives a login pop then input credentials of s4 hana api. after that it works .




we need to run both s4 hana api and odata service along with ui HOW?

thanks

Rituraj Singh

entry.title: Re: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content: Very useful blog!

entry.title: Re: Q) How to create API services and use them in SAP Dig...
entry.content:
Hi Wonpyo,

Please take a look at these blogs for an example of how to set up an external API:

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-sap/integrating-microsoft-teams-with-sap-digital-manufacturing-cloud/ba-p/13499743

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/external-api-web-service-integration-with-sap-digital-manufacturing/ba-p/13573720

In the first blog I explain how to create a webhook in microsoft teams (API) and then configure this in DM.

If you want to create you own API then what I normally do is write a Node JS application using VS Code and the Express framework and then push this into the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry service, it can then be registered in the service registry.

This tutorial show how to create and deploy a node JS app into cloud foundry https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-cf-buildpacks-node-create.html 

Let me know if you need more details.

Thanks

Kevin

entry.title: Re: How to Copy the data from Modeler > Data Foundation into Excel
entry.content:
Export feature is having various options to select each dimension , filter data etc and then export as a file (which involves many clicks and specific data selection ) .  It's good to have that feature in Data foundation itself . It would be more beneficial if there's an EXPORT button in DATA foundation something like the below option:

With one single click of EXPORT what ever data we see in flat table format, data can be downloaded further it can be checked in excel easily. Please help to move it to idea section if needed.

entry.title: Re: Transferring Measurement Document attachment to Notification
entry.content: Request to respond to this query and provide guidance

entry.title: #SAC Set dimension filter value from a dashboard with hierarchy to another table
entry.content:
Hello experts

I have a dashboards where I use an item hierarchy: when I click it I want to pass the selected value to another table (using same model) , to have as result the table filtered with the item selected from first dashboard.

I did many attempts but without result.

I created a similar script over "On select" function of first dashboard:

var sel = Chart_7.getSelections()[0];  // Chart_7 is the first dashboard

Table_10.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("dimItems", "[dimItems].[HryItemcode].[dimItems.ItemCodeMaster]");   // Table_10 is the receiver dashboard

What should I do to achieve the desidered result?

Thank you so much for your help

Adriano

entry.title: Re: Data Source and Service Selection configuration in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@Srikanth,

thanks for the reply. Following the instructions provided on the page you've linked, I've tried to connect to the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry in Eclipse. It said: "Could not find any Organization. Please contact your SAP BTP Global Account Administrator."

In the SAP BTP Cockpit, it says I don't have global accounts:

What should I do?

Best regards,

Alexander Schädlich

entry.content: Any inputs on this please?

entry.title: Re: Mass change product configuration setting in manny sales orders
entry.content: Added the "PLM Classification" and "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Master Data" tags, and removing "SAP Central Business Configuration" tag, to ensure more accurate categorization of the thread.

entry.title: Re: SAP Kyma environment ready for Production?
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

Could you please share the link of the tutorial to confirm?

I tried this one: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/deploy-nodejs-application-kyma.html and was able to access the application without any issue.

Here is my deployment descriptor:

---
apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  labels:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
  name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
spec:
  gateway: kyma-gateway.kyma-system.svc.cluster.local
  host: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
  rules:
  - accessStrategies:
    - handler: allow
    methods:
    - GET
    - POST
    - PUT
    - PATCH
    - DELETE
    - HEAD
    path: /.*
  service:
    name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    port: 8080

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  labels:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
  name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
      release: multitenancy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
        release: multitenancy
    spec:
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: PORT
          value: "8080"
        - name: TMPDIR
          value: /tmp
        image: gauravsap/multitenant-kyma-backend:v1  # replace with your Docker Hub account name
        name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
        ports:
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
          protocol: TCP
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: http
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: http
        startupProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: http
          failureThreshold: 15
          periodSeconds: 2
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 256M
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 256M
        securityContext:
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
          privileged: false
          runAsNonRoot: true
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /tmp
          name: tmp
      volumes:
      - emptyDir: {}
        name: tmp

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  labels:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
  name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
spec:
  ports:
  - port: 8080
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8080
  selector:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
status:
  loadBalancer: {}

---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  labels:
    release: multitenancy
  name: multitenancy
spec:
  ingress:
  - from:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          name: istio-system
      podSelector:
        matchLabels:
          app: istio-ingressgateway
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      release: multitenancy
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress

I would like to investigate what could be the possible issue you are facing. I just deployed the image publicly and does not need image secret. You should be able to use it.

Regarding your question, Kyma is very much ready for production and use productively by various customers. Here, I suspect some wrong configuration or tutorial might have some issue.

Thanks,

Gaurav Abbi

entry.title: SAP Jam Collaboration sunset and which products does SAP offer as alternative solutions?
entry.content:
You are an SAP customer, who is still running SAP Jam Collaboration solutions? Then this blog post is relevant for you.

SAP Jam Collaboration was SAP’s enterprise collaboration platform and modern intranet solution for a long time. Then SAP made the strategic decision to exit the standalone collaboration area and focus on the Digital Workplace Experience market with products such as SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. As a result, SAP announced already in 2021 the maintenance mode and planned sunset of SAP Jam Collaboration for beginning of 2027. This includes all SAP Jam Collaboration editions: Basic, Advanced, Advanced Plus, and Enterprise.

What is the status of SAP Jam Collaboration in 2024?

  • All SAP Jam Collaboration editions are now in maintenance mode and were removed from the SAP pricelist in 2021.
  • While no new enhancements will be made, they continue to be fully supported in accordance with the customers’ cloud contract. It is planned to sunset SAP Jam in January 2027.
  • SAP Jam Collaboration is also not available for purchase for new customers, existing customers can continue to use their SAP Jam Collaboration products for the duration of their contract.
  • If you are currently licensed for SAP Jam Collaboration Advanced, Advanced Plus or Enterprise Editions, you can renew these subscriptions provided that the end date of the contract does not exceed December 2026.
  • If you have benefited from SAP Jam Collaboration Basic Edition, that was provided complimentary to your SAP SuccessFactors subscription, you are welcome to continue to use this until your contract end date, provided that the end date of the contract does not exceed December 2026.

Note: customers will not be able to use SAP Jam Collaboration after end of life of the solution, and all systems would be removed permanently after this date. Therefore it is very important for customers to plan a migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to the alternative products before December 2026 or by the end of their cloud contract.

Is there a legal successor for SAP Jam Collaboration? What is the recommended go-to product for SAP customers ?

There is no legal successor product for SAP Jam Collaboration editions. SAP has released alternative products with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone – which provide many features known from SAP Jam Collaboration editions, but also offer a broad range of additional capabilities and innovative features. With these new products, various use cases like engaging employee experience platforms, modern intranets or integrated digital workplace scenarios can be implemented. 

Why is SAP investing into a unified workplace experience that increases engagement and productivity of end users?

When asking employees about the primary operational challenge that they encounter when trying to accomplish their day-to-day work activities, you will often get the answer that it is too hard for them find the data that they need, that they need too much time by switching between applications and finding the right applications to accomplish the tasks. The work experience is fragmented between multiple tools, tons of systems, repositories and applications. If we look at a typical SAP customer system landscape, the end users often need to access multiple SAP systems cloud and on-premise with multiple user experiences via desktop and mobile devices. SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Successfactors Work Zone offer a solution to this problem. They enable companies, IT departments and employees to improve this situation by bringing it all together and making it easier to consume. Both products deliver consistent and engaging user experiences across all channels, business processes, and applications.

What is SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP Build Work Zone empowers IT professionals and line of business experts to easily build and design engaging business sites for employees, partners and customers with intuitive drag-and-drop functionality. It connects to all systems (SAP and non-SAP) by creating a single access point to applications, processes, information and data on any device, from desktop or mobile, and it maximize team productivity by enabling guided experiences and knowledge sharing.

It is available in two editions:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, provides a central access point to multiple SAP cloud & on-premise systems, integrating apps and providing customizations and extensions.
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, provides advanced features for content management, web content, interactive and engaging workspaces, and integration with third party solutions. This image shows an example of a homepage created with the advanced edition:

For a migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to SAP Build Work Zone, the advanced edition is the correct migration path, as workspaces - the evolution of Jam groups - are only available in the advanced edition. 

See also: 

  • SAP Build Work Zone on SAP Community
  • SAP Build Work Zone on sap.com
  • FAQs

What is SAP Successfactors Work Zone and what is the difference to SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone is a comprehensive digital workplace solution offering a centralized hub for all work-related needs. From accessing information and completing tasks to learning, collaborating, and seeking support, SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone provides a single entry point for employees to easily navigate their work environment. SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone is a dedicated solution bundle with user-based subscription model. The package includes SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, plus several SAP BTP services (such as SAP Business Application Studio, Custom Domain). It includes the same feature set as the regular SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.

All business content packages (such as HR) with pre-built UI Integration Cards, workflows (guided experiences) and workspace templates are also available in both commercial options: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition as well as SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. More information: Commercial Models and Metering.

See also:
What is SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone? 
SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone on sap.com

Will there be tools available to support migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

There are different manual and API-based options to export content, details can be found here:

  • How to export a complete SAP Jam group into a zip file
  • SAP Jam - How to backup or transfer content from one instance to another

These options will remain available until the end of customer contract for SAP Jam or at the latest until the final sunset date which is planned for January 2027.

Apart from this and in case customers decide to purchase and implement either SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, an out-of-the-box migration and upgrade path is available. Details can be found in respective onboarding guides for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. During the onboarding process a configuration wizard is available, which supports the creation of a service instance based on an existing SAP Jam tenant:

Why should customers migrate to SAP Build Work Zone?

(Note that this section is also relevant for SAP Successfactors Work Zone as it includes the same feature set as SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.)

Features and Innovation
SAP Build Work Zone and especially the advanced edition will give you a lot of innovative features, which go far beyond the feature range that you know from SAP Jam Collaboration:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition includes most capabilities of SAP Jam Enterprise edition (and hence implicitly from the lower editions). It also provides many additional features not available in SAP Jam, like menu builder, UI Integration cards and guided experiences.
  • While admin areas and homepages are still part of the menu, a more flexible role-based menu builder with workpages is available. Workpages replace home & overview pages combining pre-built widgets (from SAP Jam) & Build Work Zone specific ability to add applications & UI Integration Cards.
  • Workspaces as part of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition represent the evolution of SAP Jam groups with expanded scope and capabilities. Workspace offer simplified access control while optionally allowing access to external users. Unlike groups, workspaces can also offer a true read-only experience hiding all collaborative elements/features.
  • As you know from SAP Jam, SAP Build Work Zone also supports both uploaded (Office, PDF, Video, etc.) as well as natively created (blogs, wikis, workpages, etc.) content.
  • SAP Build Work Zone provides a user profile with profile-level content, feeds & settings and always provisioned using SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • Joule integration is also planned for SAP Build Work Zone in 2024.  

Smooth migration path
Another motivation to start a migration project is the fact that the migration path from SAP Jam Collaboration to Work Zone is technically a smooth transition supported fully by tools. Also SAP IT went through this transition experience in 2022 with a large project covering 145 TB of data, 15 million documents and +150.000 Jam groups converted to workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. November 25, 2022 was the date when SAP employees were provided a new digital collaborative workplace with SAP Build Work Zone replacing SAP Jam.
The cutover to SAP Build Work Zone involved remarkably little disruption for users. The "pure" technical migration took place only over one weekend. On Fridays, users still worked with SAP Jam groups. On Monday, they logged in and had SAP Build Work Zone workspaces in place, including all the content. Straight after the migration, several thousand groups (now: workspaces) were operating smoothly, letting employees complete tasks and interact with customers, partners, and other stakeholders.

Check out this video and learn from the SAP IT team how they empowered SAP employees with a modern digital workplace. You can also learn from the SAP migration project lead, Eduardo Antunes da Cunha, about his experiences with the technical migration to SAP Build Work Zone:

Watch the video!

What are the next steps?

  • Evaluate your use cases within SAP Jam Collaboration to determine your requirements.
  • Collaborate with your account team to discuss SAP's alternative solutions to meet your requirements and business needs.
  • Plan for your migration from SAP Jam Collaboration before December 2026 or by the end of your cloud contract (whichever is the earliest date). Do not miss the date: the migration support tools require that SAP Jam Collaboration is still available at customers’ site to support a migration to SAP Build Work Zone or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone.
  • If you would like to explore the extended capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, please reach out to your account team to strategize on your options.

entry.title: #SAC Dynamic percentage calculation in table
entry.content:
Hello experts,

In SAC I have this kind of table where there at max 3 rows, and I want to calculate dinamically the variance % of the last row (in this case 2024 turnover) compared to preview one (in this case 2024 forecast)

I tried to add a row where I calculated in this moment in the formula bar what I need and it works, BUT, this table is dynamic because I can filter with a story filter the year, so in this case I selected 2024, but if select 2023 as example, the table correctly refreshes but the added row is completely wrong.

Does it exist a way to implement this and how? Alternatively how could I approch this matter?

Thank you

Adriano

entry.title: Re: Create button is Greyed-out in Manage incidents > Peo...
entry.content:
Hi @phaneendra_k ,

the "Create" button is greyed out due to lack of authorizations for creation of new Business Partners in S/4HANA. Of course, this behavior may be different, if you have enhancements in your system. If it was enabled, you would be able to create a new Business Partner record, which would then be linked to your incident record as an Injured Person (or any other role you choose).

You can also simply type the name of the person in the fields on the screen. 

 

Furthermore, if you want to add a new Injured Person, you have to use the "Add" button just above the list of Involved People.

Best regards, 
Georgi

entry.title: Re: secondary DB is not connecting on HANA studio 2.0, bu...
entry.content:
You cannot connect to a secondary HANA in an HSR setup with SQL at all (if the configuration is not Active/Active (Read Enabled)). So HANA Studio cannot connect via SQL either.  
If the HSR configuration is Active/Active (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/fe5fc53706a34048bf4a3a93a5d7c866.html?locale=en-US) the secondary SQL ports are open for read access only.
Not sure, whether HANA Studio is able to connect via SQL in this case.

entry.title: Item number is missing; specify an item number when connecting to oProductionOrders
entry.content:
Hey, I'm trying to add a draft of oInventoryGenEntry and oInventoryGenExit connected to oProductionOrders.
And the message I got is "Item number is missing; specify an item number".
The code is working for oPurchaseDeliveryNotes, oDeliveryNotes etc.
Previous message, when I provided itemcode was: The field should be empty if the document is referenced to a production order. [IGE1.ItemCode][line: 0] [Message 3559-13]

Here is the code sample:
BoObjectTypes sapType = BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry;
BoObjectTypes sapOrderType = BoObjectTypes.oProductionOrders;
var document = (Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oDrafts);
document.DocObjectCode = sapType;
I'm specifying CardCode, DocDate and everything that has to be.

here is the part about adding documentlines
if (doc1.OrderID > 0)
{
document.Lines.BaseLine =  (int)position.Ordinal;
document.Lines.BaseEntry = (int)doc1.OrderID;
document.Lines.BaseType = (int)baseType;
}
if (doc1.DocumentType.Code != "PW" && doc1.DocumentType.Code != "RW")
      document.Lines.ItemCode = position.ProductCode;
document.Lines.Quantity = Convert.ToDouble(position.Quantity);
document.Lines.WarehouseCode = position.VirtualWarehouseCode;
document.Lines.Price = Convert.ToDouble(position.Price ?? 0);

and then is also part of adding BatchNumbers and SerialNumbers.
The code is working for every draft except of oInventoryGenEntry and oInventoryGenExit.

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes it is well saved.

I used the routing scripts existing in the system like "Failure" "No open Nonconformances" "Open Nonconformances" ......

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP - Trigger workflow events upon save action in RAP Applications
entry.content:
Dears,

I have an interesting update to share. There is another option that serves the same purpose: the additional save functionality. Which uses the same implementation method save_modified.

The key difference between the unmanaged save and the additional save in the behavior definition is that the latter requires a persistent table.

Therefore, a persistent table can only be used with the additional save and not with the unmanaged save.

the new behavior definition accordingly:

managed implementation in class zbp_cds_pr_buyer_assign unique;

define behavior for ZCDS_PR_Buyer_Assign 
persistent table ZMM_PR_BUYER_BO
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
with additional save
{
  update ( features : instance, precheck );
  delete ( features : instance, precheck );

  field ( mandatory : create, readonly : update ) PurchaseRequisition;
  field ( mandatory : create ) buyer;


    field (readonly) PurchaseRequisitionItem;
    field (readonly) PurchaseRequisitionType;
    field (readonly) PurchasingDocumentTypeName;
    field (readonly) NumberOfItems;
    field (readonly) PurchasingDocumentSubtype;
    field (readonly) Currency;
    field (readonly) TotalNetAmount;
    field (readonly) Material;
    field (readonly) PurchaseRequisitionItemText;
    field (readonly) Plant;
    field (readonly) PurReqnReleaseStatus;
    field (readonly) PurchasingGroup;
    field (readonly) PurchasingGroupName;
    field (readonly) RequisitionerName;
    field (readonly) CreatedByUser;
    field (readonly) CreatedByFullName;


validation validateBuyer on save { field Buyer; create;update; }

}


Regards,

Abdelrahman Zaki

entry.title: Re: Data Source and Service Selection configuration in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@Alexander_Schaedlich , Thanks. You can go either with option Access Feature Set B or Have a SAP BTP Trail Account > Global account. You have something as below. 

In the SAP BTP cockpit, you can create a service key for the ABAP system. You can use this service key later to specify the ABAP instance to which you want to log on from the ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/setting-up-an-abap-environment-on-sap-btp/setting-up-an-abap-environment-in-sap-btp_d0e395ae-6bb3-468e-b141-0934a16c3f37 

Hope this helps. 

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: Re: sap log showing out of memory
entry.content:
Hello,

Pls check the desktop or PC and Server free memory while executing the AR log. is it possible to share the screenshot?

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: Runtime Exception ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY behaves differen...
entry.content:
Reproduced in 7.52 SP 04.

As far as I can see in the documentation of CORRESPONDING, it says that to avoid a duplicate exception (doesn't say what kind of exception it is), DISCARDING DUPLICATES is to be used.

Now, what should happen exactly if a duplicate happens, according to the documentation and is that true if I test in ABAP?

The documentation of CORRESPONDING says that the insertion behaves like MOVE-CORRESPONDING (for itabs).

The documentation of MOVE-CORRESPONDING says that "Finally, and if necessary, the table keys and associated table indexes are updated in the target table in accordance with the rules for insertions in internal tables. The corresponding exceptions are raised if uniqueness is violated."

It's still not very clear what exact rules are managed for "insertions in internal tables", it behaves differently for one line at a time and for one set of lines (LINES OF).

If you use MOVE-CORRESPONDING, you get ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY.

If you use INSERT one line without using LINES OF, the line is not inserted, SY-SUBRC is set to 4 and the program continues.

If you use INSERT LINES OF, you get ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY.

The documentation of INSERT, line_spec > LINES OF seems wrong to me: "If there is a conflict with an existing unique table key, a catchable exception is raised when inserting multiple lines from an internal table."

If you look at the general documentation of INSERT - itab, you finally obtain the correct rule lost at the bottom of the page: "Uncatchable Exceptions Cause: When inserting a set of lines, entries with an identical key were produced (the target table is defined by UNIQUE). Runtime error: ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY".

So, I guess that SAP could improve the documentation and clarify.

My tests:

DATA(itab3) = VALUE _hashed_table_type( ).
TRY.
    CASE 6.
      WHEN 1.
        " Runtime error ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        itab3 = CORRESPONDING _hashed_table_type( itab1 ).
      WHEN 2.
        " Runtime error ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 TO itab3.
      WHEN 3.
        " Runtime error ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        INSERT LINES OF itab1 INTO TABLE itab3.
      WHEN 4.
        " SY-SUBRC = 4 if duplicate
        INSERT VALUE #( id = 1 name = |Tamit| ) INTO TABLE itab3.
        INSERT VALUE #( id = 1 name = |Tamit| ) INTO TABLE itab3.
      WHEN 5.
        " CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        itab3 = VALUE _hashed_table_type( FOR <itab> IN itab1 ( CORRESPONDING #( <itab> ) ) ).
      WHEN 6.
        " Discard duplicate lines
        itab3 = CORRESPONDING _hashed_table_type( itab1 DISCARDING DUPLICATES ).
    ENDCASE.
  CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(error).
ENDTRY.

entry.title: SSO3 SPS SP02 (05/2017) -SUM - how to update the last SLSERVER02_12-80001957.SCA
entry.content:
Hello,

We installed and configured a SAP SSO3 System  1 year ago:

NW 7.5 Java SP25 - SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0  [ SPS SP02 (05/2017) ]

Checking SAP Portal Download area, I could see 3 newest components.
  SECURE LOGIN SERVER 3.0 SP02 from Patch 11 to Pach 12
  SSO AUTHENTICATION LIBRARY 3.0 from Patch 9 to Pach 12
  SAPCRYPTOLIBP from  Patch 8546 to Patch 8556

But when I run a new Maintenance Planner to update the Addon  SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0 , there is no new SPS, the last one is still  SPS SP02 (05/2017). And if I select it and go until the last step of Maintenance Planner, it is not updating the 3 components.

 

Question: Can I proceed as the following:

-To add the 3 Components last patch in my download basket

-To run Maintenance Planner to update the Addon  SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0

-On last Maintenance Planner, to select "Utilities -> Add from Download Basket" to add the 3 components to my SUM update XML Stack

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Down payment clearing Public Cloud
entry.content: Hello, your question has been forwarded to the experts. We hope to provide an answer soon. Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content:
Hi @JP ,

Yes, you could use Landed Costs to add 3rd party freight costs to your stock item.

Assuming the GRPO is already posted in the system, you just need to open the Landed Costs window, select the vendor, the broker (the carrier you'll have to pay in this case), input the freight costs on the costs tab (considering you have configured Landed Costs previously), and choose the allocation method. Post the document, run the Stock Audit Report, and see the costs added to the item(s).

Afterwards, you can create an AP invoice for the carrier, copying details from the very same Landed Cost document to finally post the freight charges.

SAP Documentation: SAP Help Portal

If you found this answer helpful, don’t forget to give kudos and mark it as an accepted solution if that is the case! Your feedback helps the entire community by making our knowledge base even bigger and valuable content more visible.👍

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Meilleur ID de parrainage Binance : I08V9SYO
entry.content: En 2024, vous pouvez bénéficier de réductions de commission sur toutes vos transactions commerciales à vie avec le code de parrainage le plus élevé de Binance, I08V9SYO. Nous vous recommandons également d'activer la réduction de commission BNB de 25 % après votre inscription. De cette façon, vous bénéficierez d’une réduction de commission élevée pouvant atteindre 40 % au total.

entry.title: Nice patch SAP! Revisiting your SAP BTP security measures after AI Core vulnerability fix
entry.content:
Dear community,

SAP recently fixed a critical vulnerability in the SAP AI Core service that could have allowed attackers to access sensitive data in the multi-tenant environment. This issue, dubbed "SAPwned", was responsibly disclosed and publicly shared on July 18 after it was patched. You can read more about it here.

Bottom line: SAP shows its commitment to security and timely patching of its cloud services. But remember, SAP BTP - like any cloud platform - is based on a shared responsibility model. That means you need to do your part to protect your data and applications too:

  • Pick secure authentication means (no Basic AUTH is not one of them!),
  • Be conscious that every endpoint exposed by SAP BTP like Microsoft365 lives on the Internet by design,
  • Scope Cloud Foundry + Kyma app access, and user roles to the minimum rights needed,
  • When using the popular” OAuth2 client credentials grant” with service keys rotate your secrets (at best automatically regularly)! Have your pick from app based solution like this, PowerShell module and blog on automatic cert renewal.
  • Establish a continuous process to harden your SAP cloud workloads. It is not a one stop shop.

Ever heard about “MFA fatigue”? Plain Multi-Factor-Authentication is not good enough anymore today. Additionally, enforce Conditional Access to SAP BTP service through integration the SAP ID Service or the SAP Identity Authentication Service with the corporate identity provider of your choice. See here how to do it with Microsoft Entra ID.

 

Second line of defense: Automatic detections based on the SAP Audit Log Service

Most of the BTP based services in the Cloud Foundry environment provided by SAP automatically write to the SAP Audit Log Service. Each service lists the standardized events that are propagated.

SAP has a nice video on the general workings of the SAP Audit Log Service on BTP.

This is a good start, but how useful are log entries that record a compromise if they are overlooked and hidden among countless normal entries?

I use the Microsoft Sentinel for SAP BTP solution - which went into General Availability state this week - as an example for running automatic detections via built-in analytic rules. Check out the alert “Failed access attempts across multiple Business Application Studio accounts” for instance. Password spray attack anyone?

Screenshot of Sentinel for SAP BTP solution content with out-of-the-box detections and workbooks

Once I have onboarded my subaccount (I named it SAP-AI-Core-playground), I can go wild on the ingested log entries, apply the threat intel functions, and built analytic rules. Let's browse the entries via the Kusto query language. The standard table SAPBTPAuditLog_CL holds all audit log info for your registered SAP BTP subaccounts:

Screenshot of simple KQL for SAP BTP

The Message contains the JSON payload BTP provides for each message as well as the involved BTP service identifier.

Looking at audit messages is nice, but you may go one step further by applying automatic action like blocking the SAP BTP users.

Below Screenshot shows the part of the process triggered by the included playbook. The SAP security team gets notified with evidence of the compromise, offering an approval option to block the user from a Microsoft Teams channel flow. Find more info here. Below screenshot shows the adaptive card with a trigger from SAP Business Suite. The same is possible with triggers coming from BTP too.

Screenshot of sap btp user block approval request to SAP security team on Microsoft Teams

 

The AI Core Service audit log entries alone are not useful

Threat protection-wise correlation with other signals in your company is required, because a single SAP AI Core event like “Successful retrieval of object store secret” does not tell you anything. See below a Kusto query working off the AI Core audit log info ingested by the Sentinel for SAP BTP solution.

Note: SAP publishes the available events for all the Cloud Foundry based services here. 

It identifies entries on my BTP subaccount related to AI Core activity and cross-references the IP address involved in the login and its country of origin. In my sample below I use the built-in function geo_info_from_ip_address() to learn if the BTP client remote address originated from Germany or not. Assumption here is that all my BTP developers are based there. Think about sanctioned countries lists etc.

 

 

 

 

//flag unexpected logins from countries other than Germany
let myBTPDevelopers = dynamic(['Germany']);
let login_messages = dynamic(['ClientAuthenticationSuccess','UserAuthenticationSuccess']);
SAPBTPAuditLog_CL 
| where SubaccountName == "SAP-AI-Core-playground"
| where Message has_any (login_messages)
| extend ip_ = tostring(Message.ip)
| extend country = geo_info_from_ip_address(ip_)['country']
| where country !in (myBTPDevelopers);

 

 

 

For a smoke test I teleported myself into the land of leprechauns🌈, steep cliffs, and mysterious celtic culture🍀 using an Azure VM. Marvel at the rule that identifies that mischieveous btp user!

Screenshot of found btp login from Ireland

 

The next sample uses the Threat Intelligence feature to verify if the BTP remote access can be traced back to a feed of known problematic IP indicators (e.g. a bot network). I maintained it on Sentinel on the Threat Management section using the IP known to BTP for my recent logins to the SAP AI Core service to trigger a result. In real life you would take the IPs from a threat intel feed of course. I don't have a bot net handy though😉.

Screenshot of Sentinel Threat Management experience

 That makes it available to my Kusto query as below. See below the screenshot of the result:

Screenshot of Kusto query result filtered by problematic IPs

 

 

 

//flag unexpected logins from IP indicators from Sentinel
let ips = ThreatIntelligenceIndicator
| distinct NetworkIP = tostring(NetworkIP);
let login_messages = dynamic(['ClientAuthenticationSuccess','UserAuthenticationSuccess']);
SAPBTPAuditLog_CL
| where SubaccountName == "SAP-AI-Core-playground"
| where Message contains "aicore" and Message has_any (login_messages)
| extend ip_ = trim(" ", replace(@"\s", "", replace(@"\r|\n|\t", "", tostring(Message.ip))))
| join kind=inner (
    ips
    | extend NetworkIP_ = trim(" ", replace(@"\s", "", replace(@"\r|\n|\t", "", tostring(NetworkIP))))
) on $left.ip_ == $right.NetworkIP_;

 

 

 

A natural next evolution of the detection would be to extend it to the "impossible travel" scenario.

These queries are simple to set up and are good to go to serve as new analytics rule on the solution, don’t you think?

Let me know what other scenarios you would like to see 😊

 

Thoughts on production readiness

SAP’s Audit Log Service is widely adopted across the SAP BTP services and foundational to the platform.

Sentinel for SAP BTP recently went into “General Availability” state, making it good to use for anyone who doesn’t like previews 😎

To create meaningful detections based on the SAP BTP audit log at minimum other sources, such as the Authorization and Trust Management service (XSUAA) must be considered. Enriching your threat signals with indicators from the rest of your IT landscape gets you from "SAP-security-acolyte"🧑🏻‍🏫 to master of disaster🥷🏼.

The built-in Sentinel for SAP playbooks use SAP BTP public APIs for automatic remediation. See the user API documentation for disabling users here.

 

Final words

Constantly staying ahead of attackers all the time is impossible. However, putting up a fight so they move on without doing more serious damage or at least being automatically informed about the incident puts you back in the driver’s seat.

The Sentinel for SAP BTP solution enables you to bring the SAP BTP audit log information for cross-correlation with your wider IT landscape to the Microsoft SIEM solution Sentinel. Furthermore, it powers automatic remediations like user block, password reset, and more.

For true confidence in drastic actions like blocking users, you require signals from as many sources as possible. Think beyond the SAP boundary and towards your complete IT landscape: Devices, endpoints, and suspicious logins etc. All of those touchpoints leave a trail of your attacker long before they reach SAP BTP, because of the prior phishing attempts or lateral movement etc. Have a look at Defender XDR for further info.

What detections are you running for your BTP landscape? Let the community know so we can learn from each other’s security practices.

Cheers

Martin

entry.title: Re: Design of the behavior handler - ABAP RESTful Program...
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer,

thank you so much for your prompt reply!

I applied your suggestion to factor out code, here is my example based on the Flight Reference Scenario where I moved the business logic related to the “Accept Travel” action to a separate class:

The parameters of the new method “accept_travel” must be of generic type data, since it is not possible to declare methods having RAP derived data types as parameters as far as I know. Therefore, each implementation of a factored-out method must include the following boilerplate code to handle the derived types properly:

I wonder if there is any possibility to avoid this boilerplate code, or at least to generate it automatically (maybe using the RAP Generator?)

Basically, I would like to replicate with RAP the BOPF design where each determination, validation, action has its own class out-of-the-box, so each developer within a team can easily work in parallel on a subset of these.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Task 7 - Using CQL in an unbound function implementation (July Developer Challenge - "Rever
entry.content: Should that be a lower case "i" in "(Id=12)"?

entry.title: Ідентифікатор найкращого реферала Binance: I08V9SYO
entry.content: У 2024 році ви можете отримувати комісійні знижки на всі свої торговельні операції на все життя за допомогою найвищого реферального коду Binance I08V9SYO. Ми також рекомендуємо активувати знижку 25% комісії BNB після реєстрації. Таким чином ви отримаєте високу комісійну знижку до 40% загалом.

entry.title: Binance beste Empfehlungs-ID: I08V9SYO
entry.content: Im Jahr 2024 können Sie mit dem höchsten Binance-Empfehlungscode I08V9SYO lebenslang Provisionsrabatte auf alle Ihre Handelstransaktionen verdienen. Wir empfehlen außerdem, den BNB-Provisionsrabatt von 25 % nach der Anmeldung zu aktivieren. Auf diese Weise erhalten Sie einen hohen Provisionsrabatt von insgesamt bis zu 40 %.

entry.title: ID de mejor referencia de Binance: I08V9SYO
entry.content: En 2024, podrá obtener descuentos en comisiones en todas sus transacciones comerciales de por vida con el código de referencia más alto de Binance, I08V9SYO. También recomendamos activar el descuento de comisión del 25% de BNB después de registrarse. De esta forma, obtendrás un alto descuento en comisiones de hasta el 40% en total.

entry.title: Binance en iyi referans kimliği: I08V9SYO
entry.content: 2024 yılında Binance'in en yüksek referans kodu olan I08V9SYO ile tüm alım satım işlemlerinizde ömür boyu komisyon indirimi kazanabilirsiniz. Ayrıca kaydolduktan sonra %25 BNB komisyon indirimini etkinleştirmenizi öneririz. Bu sayede toplamda %40'a varan yüksek komisyon indirimi elde edeceksiniz.

entry.content:
Hello,
I get the following error when I try to create the content provider:

DT and RT destinations are configured as per blog.
Any suggestion?
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: The use of "Bank" role in BP and Bank Hierarchy in S/...
entry.content:
Hi @EitaroYoshikawa, Thanks for the question! The bank hierarchy function in the Manage Bank Accounts - Bank Hierarchy View app allows customers to view banks in a hierarchical structure. This allows for a clear understanding of the relationship between different banks and bank groups. The hierarchy is constructed using business partners. And for this hierarchy to work, you have to assign banks with risk business partners and the role "Bank" (TR0703). If a bank is not assigned to a business partner with this role, it will not appear in the hierarchy. For more information about bank hierarchy, you can find additional details here. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/3fcbcc54adea6374e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

Regards,

Amy

entry.title: Bybit En Yüksek Referans Kodu: 72963
entry.content: Btbit en yüksek referans kodunuz72963 olarak belirtilmiştir. Bu kodu kullanarak Bybit borsasına kayıt olurken "Davet kodu" veya "Referans kodu" alanına 72963 yazmanız gerekmektedir. Bu kod ömür boyu geçerlidir ve BYBIT'te işlem yaparken %30 komisyon indirimi sağlar.

entry.title: Re: Delete error of local copy of app in Work Zone standard
entry.content:
Hi ReinerE,

I am also getting the same error while deleting or changing the "Semantic Object name" as well.

Did you get any solution for the same.

Thanks.

entry.title: DI ABAP CDS View reader operator pipeline Log tables in SAP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

  • Iam testing a DI pipeline with CDS View reader v2 operator as ABAP reader for the graph. Pipeline is running fine in DI but in this process, I want to understand how we can check log tables for the pipeline in SAP system.
  • For ABAP reader, SLT V2 operator in DI we can check in LTRC and LTRS for the all the details like administration data participation objects, application logs, statistics, expert functions, performance options, Rule Assignment, Table and Trigger options for source system. How can we all these details for CDS View? Do we have any separate T codes for CDS View operator?

Please help!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Memory limit in jobscheduler instance
entry.content: That was exactly what I was looking for, thank you!

entry.title: Самый высокий реферальный код Bybit: 72963
entry.content: Ваш самый высокий реферальный код Btbit указан как 72963. При регистрации на бирже Bybit по этому коду необходимо в поле «Пригласительный код» или «Реферальный код» написать 72963. Этот код действителен пожизненно и предоставляет скидку 30% на комиссию при торговле на BYBIT.

entry.title: Code de parrainage le plus élevé Bybit : 72963
entry.content: Votre code de parrainage Btbit le plus élevé est indiqué comme 72963. Lors de votre inscription sur l'échange Bybit en utilisant ce code, vous devez écrire 72963 dans le champ « Code d'invitation » ou « Code de parrainage ». Ce code est valable à vie et offre une réduction de commission de 30 % lors des transactions sur BYBIT.

entry.title: Re: sap log showing out of memory
Re: New PA is not visible V_001P_N
entry.content: yes the grouping is correct but when i save this .. it gave me a warning message "The Country Grouping of Company code has been changed *** this company code is used other pers. areas" and allowed me to savve . But the PA never came in V_0001P_ALL

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - How to estimate the remaining storage free space?
entry.content:
Hi @daniele_s ,

Thanks for your comment. The monitoring API is not yet available for external customers, it will become available soon, we plan to release a new blog post about the API and link both. The link that you provided is only internal to SAP, official documentation will be release via SAP Help Portal when GA.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Changes on existing Workfow (ECC)
entry.content:
Hello Workflow experts,

I have made changes on an existing Workflow working on Prod and I have created a new version of it. There are processes running on the old version. My question is: How can I make them run on the new created version ?

As soon as I continued running the task, I´m getting an error.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: sap log showing out of memory
entry.content: While opening the AR invoice list in sap b1 it doesn't shows anything in the list and SAP closes automatically.

entry.title: Höchster Bybit-Empfehlungcode: 72963
entry.content: Der höchste Code für Ihre Btbit-Empfehlung ist 72963. Wenn Sie sich mit diesem Code an der Bybit-Börse registrieren, müssen Sie 72963 in das Feld „Einladungscode“ oder „Empfehlungscode“ eingeben. Dieser Code ist lebenslang gültig und bietet einen Provisionsrabatt von 30 % beim Handel auf BYBIT.

entry.content: Hi Team, I can't find the instructions to set up Joule, or the setup for AI and integration into Teams, hoping someone can help

entry.title: import background element with attachment - maximum number of records
entry.content: import background element with attachment - is there a maximum number of records that may be imported in a single run?

entry.title: Re: S4HC- Create the billing document for down payment
entry.content:
Hi Haily,

there are 2 options for billing with the billing plan:

  1. down payment invoice FAZ plus closing invoice F2
  2. partial billing by billing type F2

In your example you will have 2 customer invoices:

  • one down pament request invoice (billing type FAZ) and
  • one closing invoice (billing type F2).

The down payment invoice FAZ is handled differently in FI. It leads to FI down payment request posting with special G/L indicator, means the incoming payment is posted as FI down payment. This FI posting is cleared, when creating the final invoice and post final incoming payment.

In case you want to use partial billing, you have to choose different date category/billing rule in the billing plan. In this case you have 2 customer invoices with billing type F2. But this case is not considered as down payment.

Regards, Doreen

entry.title: Re: IFlow for local SQL database server integrating using cloud connector
entry.content:
Hey @Raj_Puppala 

Thanks for your time to read this article. I have written this only for the learning purpose that how can we connect local database to SAP BTP. As a practitioner, I got to learn the steps to connect and now I can do whatever the conditions will be there to connect any local system or server.

Regards

Dushyant Garg

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content: Just tested locally, and it does not work with "Pass" and "Failure" scripts due to specificity of initiation of context parameters, however, it works with the predefined scripts "Nonconformances at Current Operation" and "No Nonconformances at Current Operation".

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Read and access restrictions to...
entry.content:
Hello Anssi,

Thank you for your question.

It is currently not possible to hide or suppress individual G/L accounts from the COA in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

You are welcome to request features on the Customer Influence Portal.

Kind regards,

Verena

entry.title: Re: Dunning of Accounts Receivable Open Item from Residual Method of Partial Payment
entry.content: Hello, your question has been forwarded to the experts. We hope to provide an answer soon. Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Order acknowledgment PDF opens in Browser in SCASE t code
entry.content: Can anybody help

entry.title: Re: Changes on existing Workfow (ECC)
entry.content:
Hello @Thierry_twist 

To my knowledge you can't. Workflows started in production before the change will continue with the old version. Only workflow started after the change will run with the new version. 

I think there is no way around that. Otherwise it would be all but impossible to keep workflow data consistent.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Good to have you here @Ivan-Zapryanov and glad that you appreciate all the great content! I'm part of the community team. Feel free to take part in other discussions as well or start one of your own. We also have a contest open at the moment over in the Career Corner.

It will be released on QRC Q3 2024 ?

Regards,

Jean-Louis

entry.title: Re: SSO3 SPS SP02 (05/2017) -SUM - how to update the last...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks.

 

I tested "Add Java Patches" on the right top corner of the screen "Select Stack Dependent and Independent file" and it proposes to add Netweaver Java Patch.

But not the Patch of the Addon SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0, like the SLSERVER02_12-80001957.SCA.

Adding manually the Addon patch like SLSERVER02_12-80001957.SCA on the last Maintenance Planner screen with  "Utilities -> Add from Download Basket" is working, they are added in the XML Stack.

 

So it is OK, but I would like to have a confirmation it is the right procedure and it is supported.

SAP didn't answer in OSS message about that (as there is "no error")

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!

Welcome @Louay_Othman!

Please be sure to subscribe to SAP Managed Tags (subscribe to your topic areas of interest).
We also have a contest open at the moment over in the Career Corner - why not take a look?!

entry.title: RESB-GPREIS decimal places at runtime while creating work order
entry.content:
When you use currency  as KRW( which is configure with no decimal point in OY04).

When we enter the purchasing data in Components and enter the price as 123 and try to save, RESB_BT table gets the data as 1.23 via standard code. The same runtime values are used further in enhancement. Why does sap give 1.23(incorrect) as it should give value as 123

entry.title: PS Text & Milestone Attachment Issue: Template creation of WBS & Project Definition
entry.content:
hello,

i am trying to create and save the wbs template (cj91) from already created project (project definition & wbs elements, "Project - U001.2403 - 25 07 2024"). and this template Project Def & WBS element "Template - CJ93 - 25 07 2024" created successfully. image attached for reference.

this already created project (project definition & wbs elements, "Project - U001.2403 - 25 07 2024") has wbs elements, milestones and ps text. image attached for reference.

when later i tried to create a new project (from copy option) at screen of CJ20N with reference to this created project template (which i already created and released). the system created the new project (project definition and wbs elements, "Project - U001.2405 - 25 07 2024") but did not copied the milestones and ps texts.

if i am missing any point while creating this wbs template, please guide me so i may then further fix it.

thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Display SLG1 Logs with Hierarchy (Application Log)
entry.content: APPL_LOG_DISPLAY is obsolete, you should use only BAL_* function modules (BAL_DB_SEARCH, BAL_DSP_LOG_DISPLAY, etc.)

entry.title: Re: Financial Statements from SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

choose in the tab "Special evaluations" Balance sheet type 2 and you get only monthly figures.

entry.content:

entry.title: Delta configurationfor Standard API in S/4 HANA cloud
entry.content:
In order to Create Delta for standard API:

Go to product master standard API in Business Accelerator Hub then click on tryout option.

 

Now, we have to configure our system environment here as shown below

 

 

 

 

 

And after successfully configuring Now you can tryout any of these operations in Standard API

 

Now in order to get delta, we have to take a delta pointer based on the fields in standard API. In this case I’m taking Lastchangedate field as delta pointer

 

And Filter out records based on delta pointer(Lastchangedate)as shown below

 

Here the logic is:   LastChangeDate ge datetime'2024-01-14T00:00:00’ And it says lastchangedate is greater than or equals to 2024-01-14 and also there are other logics as well: LastChangeDate ge datetime'2024-06-13T00:00:00' and LastChangeDate lt datetime'2024-06-14T00:00:00' and it only gives records between 14th june to 13th june .

So After initializing this logic run as shown below

 

we get records that are changed on or after 14th January 2024 in OData URL itself and it gives count of how many records are present as shown below

 

Now we can check data by taking URL till highlighted part and run OData in PowerBI

Now in Power BI, Select OData feed and add the required URL

And we can see required delta records here

entry.title: Re: New PA is not visible V_001P_N
entry.content: Hi, perhaps with screens shot of tables we will point the source of the problem

entry.title: Workaround for Retrieving Concurrent Employment Records in Story Reporting
entry.content:
Overview:


At SuccessFactors Story Reporting, we have a treasure trove of standard reports that are not just useful—they’re modifiable, which is the best part! Recently, while working on story reporting for one of our clients, we got to jazz up the Headcount and Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) report.

Report Features:


1. Headcount and FTE Data:
- The standard report is like a crystal ball for headcount and FTE, showing us what's happening based on the filters we apply.
- Normally, it gives us the headcount as of today and for the last years.

2. Enhancements:
- We had the opportunity to add even more value by including headcount and FTE data for the last 12 months.

Issue Identified:
- Concurrent Employments
- While testing the data, we stumbled upon a quirk with concurrent employment. It was like our employees had learned the art of cloning because one person with multiple roles was showing up more than once in the headcount.
- This magic trick was happening because our formulas were fetching the employee status from job information, pulling the user ID multiple times, and voilà—multiple counts for the same person!

Solution Implemented:
- Creating a Filter for Concurrent Employment:
1. We ventured into the Calculated Column section in the data source.
2. There, we created a new calculated column named "Concurrent Employment" with the following wizardry:
```
IF([Employment#Job Information#User ID] != [Person#Biographical Information#Person ID], "Secondary Employment", "Primary Employment")
```

3. This magical column was then added as a filter, sorting out the clones and making sure each employee was only counted once.

With this filter in place, we restored order to our headcount universe, ensuring accuracy and no more employee clones.

Conclusion:
By tackling the issue of concurrent employment, we enhanced the accuracy of our headcount and FTE reports, giving our client data that they could truly rely on.

We hope this solution brings a smile to your face and helps with your reporting needs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @Swetha_CH, so not sure to which of the C4C certifications you refer to exactly but these are the 2 we offer so far: 

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-sales-cloud-version-2

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-service-cloud-version-2

On the individual certification pages on your find on the top the "Sample Questions" that provide you with some sample questions to look at. In the "How to prepare" section the corresponding Learning Journeys are linked to prepare for the exam.
Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Available-to-promise & Transport Management use different goods receiving hours from BP master data
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Since the beginning of the year, we have been using the embedded SAP Transportation Management System. We have implemented SAP Note 1763253 to consider the BP goods receiving hours instead of the calendar resource.

Now our SAP users complain that they have to maintain the goods receiving hours in two different tabs in the Business Partner. 

For the Available-to-promise check they have to maintain the goods receiving hours under business partner role ‘Customer’ tab ‘Customer: Unloading Points’.

 

For the Transportation Management System they have to maintain the goods receiving hours under role ‘General Business Partner’ tab ‘Control’. In thise area we have far more options than under 'Customer: Unloading Points'. Here, for example, we can maintain exceptions.

 

Is there a solution from SAP to ensure that the ATP and the TM work smoothly together? It is currently very unsatisfactory that different master data objects are used. Unfortunately, this leads to mistakes and problems in users day-to-day work.

entry.title: backup sap s/4 hana cloud db
entry.content:
hello experts,

after onprem ERP moved to SAP s/4 hana cloud (as part of SAP RISE), how can i integrate my custom scripts for the backup and restore of the hana database and datalake (and write to S3)?

appreciate your response.
